---
title: "VoxelSeriesStore"
subtitle: "The storage protocol behind dense, sparse, mapped, and file-backed 4-D containers."
sidebar: concepts
---

## What it is

`VoxelSeriesStore` is a small structural protocol — five members:
`shape`, `dtype`, `series(...)`, `volume(i)`, `as_matrix()` — defined
in `neuroim.storage`. It is the *storage* contract: a uniform way to
pull a voxel's series, a timepoint's volume, or the whole time-major
matrix, regardless of how the bytes are physically held.

It is `@runtime_checkable`, so `isinstance(obj, VoxelSeriesStore)` is
a valid structural test.

## Conformance is via the adapter

The shipped containers — `DenseNeuroVec`, `SparseNeuroVec`,
`BigNeuroVec`, `MappedNeuroVec`, `FileBackedNeuroVec` — do **not**
themselves pass `isinstance(vec, VoxelSeriesStore)`. The conformer is
`NeuroVecStoreAdapter`, one frozen wrapper that spans every backend
because the per-backend behaviour lives in the wrapped vec's own
`series` / `__getitem__`. `DenseStore`, `SparseStore`, `BigStore`,
`FileBackedStore`, `MappedStore` are all aliases of that one adapter.

```python
from neuroim.storage import VoxelSeriesStore, NeuroVecStoreAdapter

isinstance(bold, VoxelSeriesStore)                        # False
isinstance(NeuroVecStoreAdapter(bold), VoxelSeriesStore)  # True
```

## Two layers, kept distinct

- **Storage layer (this protocol).** Five members. "One protocol,
  every backend" is true *here*, through the single adapter.
- **Analysis layer (separate).** `series_roi`, `temporal_snr`,
  `searchlight_apply` behaving identically across dense/sparse/mapped/
  file-backed inputs is a related guarantee carried by the `NeuroVec`
  subclasses and their shared contract layer — **not** by this
  protocol's five members. The protocol provides the storage surface
  the analysis layer is built on; it does not itself provide the
  analysis surface.

## Invariants

- **`shape` is a 4-tuple** `(nx, ny, nz, nt)`.
- **`as_matrix()` is time-major** — shape `(nt, n_voxels)`,
  Fortran-flattened voxel order. A conforming backend must preserve
  this orientation.
- **Off-grid access raises.** `series` on an off-grid voxel/index
  raises `IndexError` per [`IDX-OOB`](contract-failures.qmd). A
  backend that returns wrong-but-plausible bytes instead is
  non-conforming even if structurally a `VoxelSeriesStore`.
- **Multi-source backends enforce frame consistency.**
  `FileBackedNeuroVec` validates that every per-timepoint file's
  affine agrees; drift raises `Volume {idx} has inconsistent
  affine/space: …` before a reduction averages two frames. Backed by
  [scenario S12](https://github.com/bbuchsbaum/neuroim-python/blob/main/examples/scenarios/12_file_backed_affine_drift/REPORT.md) (PAIN-12, closed).

## What it doesn't do

- **No magic format dispatch.** Backends are constructed explicitly
  (`FileBackedNeuroVec(paths)`, `MappedNeuroVec(path, space)`).
  `read_image` returns dense storage by default; richer backends are
  explicit caller decisions.
- **No inheritance hierarchy.** Conformance is structural, not via a
  shared base class.
- **No concurrency promise.** Concurrent writes into a
  `MappedNeuroVec` are undefined. The protocol covers the read-side
  storage surface, not write-side concurrency.

## See also

- [Spec: VoxelSeriesStore protocol](../spec/voxel-series-store-protocol.html) — the third-party-implementable contract.
- [Reference: DenseNeuroVec](../reference/DenseNeuroVec.qmd), [SparseNeuroVec](../reference/SparseNeuroVec.qmd) — generated entries for the shipped backends.
- [Contract failures](contract-failures.qmd) — the `IDX-OOB` / `FB-DRIFT` refusals.
- Scenarios: [S08](https://github.com/bbuchsbaum/neuroim-python/blob/main/examples/scenarios/08_pickle_multiprocessing_handoff/REPORT.md), [S12](https://github.com/bbuchsbaum/neuroim-python/blob/main/examples/scenarios/12_file_backed_affine_drift/REPORT.md).
